title: Impacts of transportation network companies on urban mobility
data_sources: FHWA National Performance Management Research Data Set (NPMRDS)

data_scope.note: 174 MSAs with TNC operation as of 2016 and monthly PT ridership data; 44 MSAs for congestion models (NPMRDS coverage)

core_conclusion: TNCs (Uber, Lyft) intensified US urban transport challenges: +0.9% road congestion intensity, +4.5% congestion duration, -8.9% public transit ridership, and no significant reduction in vehicle ownership on average. TNCs primarily substitute for public transit rather than private cars, while deadheading miles compound road traffic at low occupancy.
why_it_matters: First comprehensive US-wide causal panel study quantifying TNC effects on all three mobility dimensions simultaneously; refutes the sustainability narrative; reveals temporal escalation of effects; identifies policy levers for regulatory response.
why_nature: Overturns a widely-held sustainability belief with rigorous causal evidence at national scale; immediate policy relevance for a globally active sector; methodological rigor (IV/2SLS) raises evidentiary standard; integrated multi-dimensional systems view aligns with Nature Sustainability's mandate.
added_value_to_my_research: Fine-grained spatiotemporal TNC-transit substitution surfaces from mobile/smart-card fusion -> dynamic R2SFCA transit accessibility estimator
Interact TNC treatment with socioeconomic variables -> causal estimates of how TNCs affect healthcare accessibility gaps in low-income areas
SVAR/Granger-causal framework -> bidirectional TNC-congestion-transit feedback for physics-informed spatiotemporal GNN
Map congestion increases to excess VMT/emissions -> EV charging siting as complementary policy instrument (multi-objective optimization)
Embed paper's substitution coefficient into multimodal network model + Monte Carlo -> transit resilience surface under TNC penetration scenarios

related.title: Uncovering latent urban mobility patterns via smart-card and survey data fusion
related.relation: fine-grained multimodal OD flows for disaggregating MSA-level TNC effects

related.title: Impacts of remote work on vehicle miles traveled and transit ridership in the USA
related.relation: sister panel study applying similar IV/fixed-effect approach to another urban mobility shock (remote work vs. TNCs)

related.title: Bidirectional yet asymmetric causality between urban systems and traffic dynamics in 30 cities worldwide
related.relation: extends causal identification to bidirectional congestion-urban system feedback loop

related.title: The effect of sustainable mobility transition policies on cumulative urban transport emissions and energy demand
related.relation: connects TNC-induced congestion to downstream emissions externality and policy levers

related.title: Interconnectedness enhances network resilience of multimodal public transportation systems for Safe-to-Fail urban mobility
related.relation: multimodal network resilience framework for embedding TNC as a dynamic substitution layer

figures.caption: Multiple pathways of TNCs' impact on urban mobility — causal diagram showing substitution from transit, low-occupancy deadheading, and congestion feedback loops
